title: Exploring the trade-offs between spatial and spectral resolution in mapping invasive alien trees
authors: Thandeka Skosana; Karen Joan Esler; Alanna Jane Rebelo
dataset_name: BioSCape Raw Training Dataset (Stellenbosch University ScholarData); EMIT hyperspectral data (NASA); iNaturalist BioSCape Alien Mapping Project
notes: Example article. Compares EMIT hyperspectral/Sentinel-2/SPOT6 for mapping Pinus/Acacia/Eucalyptus/Hakea/Populus in Cape Floristic Region. Data availability section references Stellenbosch ScholarData and iNaturalist. SSRN preprint: https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5226584

title: Mapping invasive alien trees in water towers: A combined approach using satellite data fusion, drone technology and expert engagement
authors: Petra B. Holden; Alanna J. Rebelo; Mark G. New
journal: Remote Sensing Applications: Society and Environment
dataset_name: Field GPS points; Sentinel-1+2 imagery; drone data (not publicly shared)
notes: Maps invasive trees (pines/gums/wattles) in Berg and Breede catchments (SW Cape) using GEE. Training data from field + drone; not publicly deposited.

title: Can Sentinel-2 be used to detect invasive alien trees and shrubs in Savanna and Grassland Biomes?
authors: Alanna J. Rebelo; Shaeden Gokool; Petra B. Holden; Mark G. New
journal: Remote Sensing Applications: Society and Environment
dataset_name: Sentinel-2/Sentinel-1/landform field-collected training data (uMngeni Catchment KwaZulu-Natal)
notes: Maps gums/wattles/pines/bugweed in uMngeni Catchment (~17.5% invaded). Training data collected in field; no public data deposit stated.

title: BioSCape Invasive Alien Plant map for the Cape Floristic Region (dataset)
authors: Alanna J. Rebelo; Nadia Coertze; Anthony G. Rebelo
dataset_name: BioSCape IAP classification map (10m raster) + training data (field + iNaturalist 2023-2024)
notes: Random Forest 10m map of Eucalyptus/Hakea/Pinus/Populus/Acacia/other across CFR. Training data submitted to iNaturalist BioSCape Alien Mapping Project. 93% overall accuracy.

title: BioSCape Raw Training Dataset
authors: Alanna J. Rebelo; Nadia Coertze; Anthony G. Rebelo
dataset_name: Raw field + iNaturalist training occurrence points for 6 IAP classes
notes: Companion training dataset to the BioSCape CFR map. Includes GPS occurrence polygons/points for IAP taxa. Downloadable.

title: MapWAPS Invasive Alien Plant map for the Tugela Catchment (dataset)
authors: Alanna J. Rebelo et al. (ARC MapWAPS project)
dataset_name: Random Forest IAP classification map + training data shapefiles (Tugela Catchment KwaZulu-Natal)
notes: 10m Sentinel-2 RF map. Training data in shapefile format available for download.

title: MapWAPS Invasive Alien Plant Map for the uMzimvubu Catchment (dataset)
authors: Alanna J. Rebelo et al. (ARC MapWAPS project)
dataset_name: Random Forest IAP classification map + training data shapefiles (uMzimvubu Catchment Eastern Cape)
notes: 10m Sentinel-2 RF map.

title: MapWAPS Invasive Alien Plant Map for the Luvuvhu Catchment (dataset)
authors: Alanna J. Rebelo et al. (ARC MapWAPS project)
dataset_name: Random Forest IAP classification map + training data shapefiles (Luvuvhu Catchment Limpopo)
notes: 10m Sentinel-2 RF map.

title: MapWAPS Invasive Alien Plant map for the Sabie-Crocodile Catchments (dataset)
authors: Alanna J. Rebelo et al. (ARC MapWAPS project)
dataset_name: Random Forest IAP classification map + training data shapefiles (Sabie-Crocodile Mpumalanga)
notes: 10m Sentinel-2 RF map.

title: Sentinel-2 time series based optimal features and time window for mapping invasive Australian native Acacia species in KwaZulu Natal, South Africa
authors: Cecilia Masemola; Moses Azong Cho; Abel Ramoelo
journal: International Journal of Applied Earth Observation and Geoinformation
dataset_name: Sentinel-2 time-series; field occurrence data for Acacia mearnsii and A. dealbata (Van Reenen KwaZulu-Natal)
notes: Identifies optimal S2 bands/dates for Acacia spp. mapping. Field data collected; no public deposit stated.

title: Towards a semi-automated mapping of Australia native invasive alien Acacia trees using Sentinel-2 and radiative transfer models in South Africa
authors: Cecilia Masemola; Moses Azong Cho; Abel Ramoelo
journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
dataset_name: Sentinel-2; PROSAIL RTM; field occurrence data (Acacia mearnsii and A. dealbata KwaZulu-Natal)
notes: PDF available from UP institutional repository. No public occurrence dataset deposit found.

title: Remotely sensed characterization of Acacia longifolia invasive plants in the Cape Floristic region of the Western Cape, South Africa
authors: Siedit; co-authors not confirmed in search results
dataset_name: Sentinel-2; field occurrence data for Acacia longifolia (Cape Floristic Region Western Cape)
notes: Maps A. longifolia at catchment scale; ~10% of catchment invaded. 88.7% accuracy. No public occurrence data deposit found.

title: Mapping an alien invasive shrub within conservation corridors using super-resolution satellite imagery
authors: K Jurie Theron; James S. Pryke; Nicolas Latte; Michael J. Samways
dataset_name: Sentinel-2 super-resolution; field occurrence data for American bramble (Rubus cuneifolius) KwaZulu-Natal
notes: Maps bramble in commercial timber/grassland mosaic using deep learning super-resolution. Training/occurrence data not publicly deposited.

title: Remote sensing of invasive alien wattle using image texture ratios in the low-lying Midlands of KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa
authors: Kiara Brewer; Romano Lottering; Kabir Peerbhay
journal: Remote Sensing Applications: Society and Environment
dataset_name: SPOT-6 imagery; field occurrence data for Acacia mearnsii (KwaZulu-Natal Midlands)
notes: Uses image texture ratios + SPLS-DA for wattle detection. Field GPS occurrence data not publicly shared.

title: Modeling the geographic spread and proliferation of invasive alien plants (IAPs) into new ecosystems using multi-source data and multiple predictive models in the Heuningnes catchment, South Africa
authors: B. Mtengwana; T. Dube; B.T. Mudereri; C. Shoko
dataset_name: Sentinel-2; bioclimatic variables; IAP occurrence data (Heuningnes Catchment Western Cape)
notes: MaxEnt/RF/BRT SDMs for IAP spread prediction. Occurrence data sources not explicitly stated as downloadable.

title: Use of multispectral satellite datasets to improve ecological understanding of the distribution of Invasive Alien Plants in a water-limited catchment, South Africa
authors: B. Mtengwana; T. Dube; Y.P. Mkunyana; D. Mazvimavi
dataset_name: Landsat-8; Sentinel-2; field occurrence data (Cape Agulhas Western Cape)
notes: Centre for Invasion Biology Zenodo community noted: https://zenodo.org/communities/cib/. No specific dataset DOI for this paper.

title: Potential distribution of selected invasive alien plants under current and future climate change scenarios in South Africa
authors: Achamyeleh G. Mengistu; Weldemichael A. Tesfuhuney; Yali E. Woyessa; Abraham S. Steyn
dataset_name: GBIF occurrence data for Prosopis spp (6064 records) and Acacia mearnsii (2779 records); WorldClim CMIP6
notes: Uses GBIF occurrence download. Data available on request from authors.

title: Remote Sensing of Invasive Lantana camara (Verbenaceae) in Semiarid Savanna Rangeland Ecosystems of South Africa
authors: Timothy Dube; Cletah Shoko; Mbulisi Sibanda; Paschaline Madileng; Xivutiso G. Maluleke; Velma R. Mokwatedi; Lorencia Tibane; Tebogo Tshebesebe
dataset_name: Landsat-8 OLI; Sentinel-2 MSI; field occurrence data for Lantana camara (KwaZulu-Natal semi-arid savanna)
notes: First comparative assessment of Landsat-8 vs Sentinel-2 for Lantana mapping in SA. 78.4% accuracy with S2. No public data deposit.

title: Detecting and mapping the spatial distribution of Chromoleana odorata invasions in communal areas of South Africa using Sentinel-2 multispectral remotely sensed data
authors: Authors not confirmed in searches
journal: Physics and Chemistry of the Earth Parts A/B/C
dataset_name: Sentinel-2; field occurrence data for Chromoleana odorata (communal areas South Africa)
notes: Maps Siam weed invasion using Sentinel-2 + Random Forest. Field GPS occurrence data not publicly shared.

title: Exploring the capability of high-resolution satellite data in delineating the potential distribution of common invasive alien plant species in the Tshivhase Tea Estate
authors: F. Nembambula; O.E. Malahlela; L. Mugwedi
dataset_name: SPOT-7; Sentinel-2; field occurrence data for Solanum mauritianum/Lantana camara/Chromolaena odorata (Tshivhase Limpopo)
notes: Tests SPOT-7 and S2 for mapping 3 IAP species co-occurrence. No public data deposit found.

title: Changes in the cover of selected invasive alien plant taxa between 2008 and 2023 in South Africa
authors: Johann D.F. Kotzé; Andrew Wannenburgh; Brian W. van Wilgen
dataset_name: National survey plot data 47830 points (2008 and 2016-2023); Sentinel-2 ancillary
notes: Data in possession of Kotzé and currently NOT publicly available. National-scale survey of 14 IAP taxa.

title: Mapping Ecologically Disruptive Invasive Acacia Species Using EnMAP Hyperspectral and Sentinel-1 Radar Data
authors: Authors at University of Johannesburg (confirmed affiliation only)
dataset_name: EnMAP hyperspectral (DLR 2022); Sentinel-1 SAR; field occurrence points for Acacia cyclops and A. mearnsii (Cape Floristic Region)
notes: EnMAP scene 31 Aug 2022. 83-88% accuracy for 2 Acacia spp. Article is open access; check supplementary for data links.

title: Invasive, naturalized and casual alien plants in southern Africa: A summary based on the Southern African Plant Invaders Atlas (SAPIA)
authors: L. Henderson
dataset_name: SAPIA database - 548 naturalized/casual species; ~60000 quarter-degree locality records
notes: Key foundational SAPIA paper. Database accessible via ARC-PPRI and GBIF (DOI:10.15468/pkgevu). CC-BY 4.0 article.

title: Changes in the composition and distribution of alien plants in South Africa: An update from the Southern African Plant Invaders Atlas
authors: L. Henderson; J.A. Wilson
dataset_name: SAPIA database update to 2016 - 773 alien taxa; records for 50000+ locality squares
notes: Updated SAPIA summary with distribution maps. SAPIA data accessible via ARC on request and via GBIF.

title: CABI Africa Invasive and Alien Species data (GBIF occurrence dataset)
authors: A. Witt; T. Beale (CABI); L. Henderson (SAPIA coordinator)
dataset_name: ~87000 georeferenced occurrence records for 773 alien plant taxa in southern Africa
notes: Directly downloadable from GBIF. Roadside surveys + SAPIA records. Key SA occurrence source.

title: Deriving inventories of non-native plant species from iNaturalist: Insights from urban centres of the Western Cape, South Africa
authors: C.P. Gildenhuys; L.J. Potgieter; C. Hui; D.M. Richardson
dataset_name: iNaturalist occurrence records for non-native plants; Western Cape urban centres
notes: Uses iNaturalist as primary occurrence source. ANNIC workflow. Open access.

title: Detecting Invasive Alien Plant Species Using Remote Sensing, Machine Learning and Deep Learning
authors: Perry B. Rakgoale; Silas Njoya Ngetar
dataset_name: Review of published datasets and methods - no primary occurrence data
notes: Systematic review of 1689 articles on RS/ML/DL for IAP detection. South Africa-based authors (UKZN). No primary dataset.

title: Mapping invasive alien trees in grassland ecosystems using airborne imaging spectroscopy and vegetation functional traits - SAEON/BioSCape airborne data South Africa
authors: Multiple - BioSCape campaign team
dataset_name: AVIRIS-NG airborne hyperspectral imagery (Oct-Nov 2023); field occurrence data from BioSCape campaign
notes: NASA BioSCape airborne campaign Oct-Nov 2023 collected AVIRIS-NG hyperspectral + HyTES + PRISM data over CFR. Data being published. See https://www.bioscape.io for data access.
